_doc: === PARTICIPATION: Links an entity to a situation with role and context ===
_entity_id: REQUIRED. UUID of the participating entity.
_situation_id: REQUIRED. UUID of the situation the entity participates in.
_role: OPTIONAL (default: Protagonist). One of: Protagonist, Antagonist, Witness, Target, Instrument, Confidant, Informant, Recipient, Bystander, SubjectOfDiscussion, or any custom string via Custom("role-name").

knows_before.fact: Meridian Trading is a front company

info_set._knows_before: What the entity knows BEFORE this situation begins. Array of {about_entity: UUID, fact: string, confidence: float}.

learns.fact: Meridian's financial records show money laundering

info_set._learns: What the entity LEARNS during this situation.
info_set._reveals: What the entity REVEALS to others during this situation.
info_set._beliefs_about_others: OPTIONAL. Recursive beliefs: 'A thinks B knows X'. Depth-2 epistemic reasoning. last_updated_at is the situation UUID where this belief was last revised.

_info_set: OPTIONAL. Epistemic state — what the entity knows, learns, reveals, and believes others know. The foundation for information-asymmetry analysis.
_action: OPTIONAL. Short description of what the entity does in this situation.
_payoff: OPTIONAL. Game-theoretic payoff — arbitrary JSON representing the entity's outcome.
_seq: OPTIONAL (default: 0). Sequence number for multi-role participation. An entity can have multiple roles in the same situation (e.g., both Protagonist and Informant) — each role gets a different seq.

_doc: === PARTICIPATION: Organization as subject of discussion ===
_role_note: SubjectOfDiscussion means the entity is talked about but not physically present.
Locations typically participate as Instrument (setting/backdrop) or Target.
